source.name: gstack by Garry Tan
source.license_notes: MIT — role abstractions generalized, not copied verbatim

goal: Design multi-role AI agent teams where each role represents a quality checkpoint with defined responsibility boundaries, structured checklists, and deliverable standards — not simulating humans but embedding engineering discipline.
when_to_use: Complex projects requiring multiple review perspectives
inputs_required: Available AI models for cross-model review
steps: Identify required quality gates for the project type
Define each gate's checklist and stop condition
Set the gate execution order (sequential, not parallel)
Execute gates at the correct stage (Plan/Review/Test/Ship)
api_calls: POST /brief to retrieve domain rules for gate criteria
prompt_contract: Roles are quality gates, not personality simulations
Each role has a defined boundary and checklist
Cross-model agreement is signal, not proof — user has final say
Not all 23 roles apply to every project — select based on project needs
verification: Each required gate has been executed
Gate results documented with pass/fail and evidence
Failed gates have been resolved or escalated
failure_modes: Applying all 23 roles to a simple script (overhead)
Using role names without defining actual checklists
handoff_report: List which gates were used and results

quality.notes: Abstracted from gstack's 23-role team design. The key insight: roles are quality gates, not people. Adapted to be platform-agnostic.

retrieval.ai_summary: Multi-role AI agent team design pattern where each role (CEO/Eng/Design/DX/QA/Security/Release) represents a quality checkpoint with defined checklists. Roles are quality gates, not personality simulations. Select roles based on project needs.
